programmes.show_description: How do they make the favourite sweet of the Ottomans, James Bond's gyroplane and the Supercat- the world's fastest ferry? S13, Ep1
How do they carve longboards, turn millions of lemons into juice, and build industry's versatile heavy lifter, the forklift truck? S13, Ep2
How do they roast the coffee that wakes up Italy, bake the bricks that build America, and blast diamonds from a frozen volcano? S13, Ep3
How do they take the world's sharpest photos, stitch an airbag to catch a falling car, and produce billions of perfectly-formed jelly beans? S13, Ep4
DeLorean. Mike has travelled to the US to hunt for a DeLorean, one of the most recognisable and distinctive cars ever made. Can Edd restore this iconic car and maximise their profit? S8, Ep7
Chevy Truck. Mike is in San Francisco looking for a classic Chevrolet Step Side Pick Up. Will he manage to find a rust-free model for Edd to restore? S8, Ep8
Across The Irish Sea. The adventure in Northern Ireland continues. Henry and Sam hunt down some army vehicles, tractors and some very well-hidden land rovers. S2, Ep28
A Royal Visit. Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove are in the Queen's realm as they visit a shed in the shadow of Windsor Castle. Will they find a royal bargain of a motorcycle? S2, Ep29
Discover how they manufacture bicycle helmets, produce aluminium, create lithium batteries and make car brakes. S2, Ep19
Find out how lenses, granite, crisps and computer processors are produced, as more everyday objects are put under the microscope. S2, Ep20
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
The team restore a miniature whiskey barrel. Plus, a heritage craft dating back thousands of years is put to good use repairing a set of wicker baby-weighing scales. S4, Ep22
Forgotten Treasures. Drew snaps up a rare pair of console tables in Sherborne. Then, he visits the UK's last drop hammer tool-making forge before picking in the Cotswolds. S19, Ep10
Key Hunting Grounds. Drew takes Viki to his favourite hunting grounds. Together, they pick through a dusty old shed, a quality auction, and a beautiful country house. S19, Ep11
A wet plant disaster threatens the Dust Devils' potentially lucrative fine gold find. Plus, the Gold Timers desperately try to turn their season around. S5, Ep9
The Gold Gypsies could be sitting on a fortune but must decide whether to take a huge gamble. A water shortage halts Shane and Russell's operations. S5, Ep10
Northumberland sees Natasha on safari tracking ancient creatures while Steven finds an old elephant. Both experts go Georgian with their treasures in Dundee. S22, Ep17
After clearing out a home in Leeds, the team sort through a van full of mysterious boxes. Plus, Angus checks out a retired archaeologist's beautiful collection. S2, Ep7
The team restore a miniature whiskey barrel. Plus, a heritage craft dating back thousands of years is put to good use repairing a set of wicker baby-weighing scales. S4, Ep22
Penny For Your Thoughts. Back in Northern Ireland, Henry and Sam discover some lovely vintage cars as well as some modern motors. What will they take home? S2, Ep30
Henry and Sam are in the Cotswolds to visit the largest sidecar manufacturer in Britain. They uncover a rusty Lancia Aurelia and a Royal Enfield 500. S3, Ep1
Graham Streeter. Henry and Fuzz visit Graham's Essex shed, packed with bikes, and uncover a Suzuki GSX-R750 Slabside, Henry's dream machine and a true Japanese icon. S7, Ep13
Jeff Maggs. Henry and Fuzz visit Jeff's tractor-packed shed in Chepstow and uncover a Grey Fergie, the iconic Ferguson TE20 that helped transform farming forever. S7, Ep14
Dominic Littlewood & Lisa George. In Dom Littlewood's stylish seaside pad, Angus finds a rare guitar. Then, he checks out 1950s treasures at soap star Lisa George's home. S4, Ep5
Les Dennis & Sherrie Hewson. Angus unearths some family fortunes at comedy legend Les Dennis' home. Then, he checks out a selection of movie memorabilia at Sherrie Hewson's house. S4, Ep6
Charles Hanson and Catherine Southon resume their trip in Warwickshire. Catherine finds a French copper and brass letter holder and Charles grabs a charming oak chair. S14, Ep3
Charles Hanson and Catherine Southon embark on the penultimate leg of their trip. Charles goes to work with a metal detector while Catherine sticks to antiques stores. S14, Ep4
Rick Kenway. Henry and Fuzz visit Richard's eclectic shed in Hook, packed with bikes, cars and curiosities, and choose a classic Harley-Davidson Sportster to save. S7, Ep15
Dave McNicholas. Henry and Fuzz visit Dave's Derby shed and pick a classic Triumph Daytona, a British icon with racing heritage, for their next restoration challenge. S7, Ep16
Heading In The Right Direction. The Cogs take centre stage at Britain's premiere car show, but the wheels fall off when Richard returns James May's restored Triumph Stag. S5, Ep8
Rio Whistler. Henry and Fuzz visit James in Burnham-on-Crouch to revive his rare Rio Whistler military vehicle. Can they return this weathered veteran to duty? S4, Ep2
How do they build a caravan, forge tuning forks, resurface an ice rink, and put on the world's biggest light festival? S13, Ep5
How do they make the groovy vinyl LP? How do they build the dodgem? How do they make one of the world's oldest pain remedies, tiger balm? S13, Ep6
How do they brew a beer that won't make you drunk? How do they build a car that flies over rocks? How are escalators made? How do lie detectors work? S13, Ep7
How do they forge the axe? How do they put together a courier's messenger bag, and how are the fastest lifeboats built? S13, Ep8
Karmann Ghia. Mike is in Idaho to buy the ultimate retro sportster, the Karmann Ghia. Edd loves it, but will the broken gearbox and shoddy interior stop them making a profit? S8, Ep9
Bel Air. Mike gets a great deal on a classic '57 Chevy Bel Air, and Edd does a great job on restoring it - but have they made an expensive mistake with their colour choice? S8, Ep10
Penny For Your Thoughts. Back in Northern Ireland, Henry and Sam discover some lovely vintage cars as well as some modern motors. What will they take home? S2, Ep30
Henry and Sam are in the Cotswolds to visit the largest sidecar manufacturer in Britain. They uncover a rusty Lancia Aurelia and a Royal Enfield 500. S3, Ep1
Find out how jet skis, wine, office furniture and ice skates are made, as more everyday objects are put under the microscope. S2, Ep21
Find out how winter jackets, animations, mushrooms and gold rings are produced, as more everyday objects are put under the microscope. S2, Ep22
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Susie is challenged with restoring a leather pouffe brought back from Egypt after WWII. Plus, Will is tasked with fixing a model Spitfire with an emotional backstory. S4, Ep23
Long Running Businesses. Drew and Al pick through the collection of a travelling fairground family. Then, they are invited to a traditional wood-turning business. S19, Ep12
Buying At The Right Price. Drew and Viki deal with a haggling Blacksmith in Yorkshire. Then, they visit a 19th-century country mansion on the south coast of Scotland. S19, Ep13
The Goldtimers hire a 7-tonne loader to end their gold drought. The Dust Devils risk running out of water. Marcus vanishes while clifftop detecting. S5, Ep11
The Poseidon Crew hunts for bush gold. The Gold Gypsies face a tough decision. The Victoria Diggers' perseverance could finally pay off. S5, Ep12
Steven and Natasha travel to the Scottish Borders in their 1960s Rolls Royce. They reveal the hidden past of the Roman Empire and discover an antique hiding a secret. S22, Ep18
In Knaresborough, Angus sorts through a massive collection of model aircrafts and incredible paintings. Later, Angus auctions off some iconic toys. S2, Ep8
Susie is challenged with restoring a leather pouffe brought back from Egypt after WWII. Plus, Will is tasked with fixing a model Spitfire with an emotional backstory. S4, Ep23
In Somerset, Sam and Henry meet a man who's been collecting cars since 1963. The guys are excited to uncover an Austin 7 and a De Dion-Bouton. S3, Ep2
Sam and Henry visit one of Britain's biggest dealers in second-hand milk floats. This quirky collection features a beautiful 1959 Chevrolet El Camino. S3, Ep3
Rick Kenway. Henry and Fuzz visit Richard's eclectic shed in Hook, packed with bikes, cars and curiosities, and choose a classic Harley-Davidson Sportster to save. S7, Ep15
Dave McNicholas. Henry and Fuzz visit Dave's Derby shed and pick a classic Triumph Daytona, a British icon with racing heritage, for their next restoration challenge. S7, Ep16
Colin Jackson & Phil Daniels. Angus helps Phil Daniels with 1960s and 70s goodies in his Yorkshire home. Then, he finds the 2012 Olympic torch in Colin Jackson's family home. S4, Ep7
Angus helps Claire Richards by taking a huge chunk of pop memorabilia to auction. And he finds a blunderbuss and chastity belt in Amanda Owen's home. S5, Ep1
Charles Hanson and Catherine Southon begin the final leg of their trip. Charles finds a collection of railwayana before the auction in Congleton. S14, Ep5
Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey set off on a south coast antiques hunt. Starting in Littlehampton, they travel north to an auction in Merseyside. S14, Ep6
John Sheppard. Henry and Fuzz head to Towcester to buy John's Allis-Chalmers crawler. They are determined to revive this classic machine. S7, Ep17
Ollie Turney. Henry and Fuzz visit Ollie to inspect a tired 1955 BSA bike. Though faded and neglected, this legendary machine still boasts heaps of charm. S7, Ep18
Rock Relics & Robbery Files. Irita discovers a stash of Great Train Robbery papers. Plus, she sells a grandad's 6000 comics and rare coins to fund his baby granddaughter's future. S3, Ep1
To Be Cherished By Others. Angus attempts to find new homes for the beloved belongings of a departed father. Then, he helps a widow clear her late-husband's collection. S3, Ep13
